title: Adnerem
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}}

{{Quote|Two Adnerem are a party, four a dinner and six a funeral.|Common phrase relating to Adnerem|<ref name="AE" />}}
The '''Adnerem''' were a tall, thin, [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Mammal/Legends|mammal]] native to the pleasure [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Adner]] in the [[Yataga sector/Legends|Yataga sector]] of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]]. Descended from [[Scavenger/Legends|scavenger]]-[[Hunting/Legends|hunters]], they had large triangular heads with wide [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and a large lump in the center of the forehead called an [[echo chamber]]. Their thin [[Limb/Legends|arms]] each ended in four talons with no opposable thumb. Adnerem society was based on small, non-genetic social units known as steri, the singular of which was [[steris]], who dwelt together in [[steris-house]]s. 

The members of a steris were known as [[sterisi]], and individual couples who formed a close friendship within a steris were known as a [[sterika]]. An Adnerem's status was judged entirely by the status of his or her steris, and different steri sometimes battled for balance of power in a conflict known as a [[raid-war]].

As a species, Adnerem were generally antisocial and remained independent from the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] economy, although their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was a tourist trap with many theme parks and [[Casino/Legends|casinos]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the largest of these casinos, [[the Red Lisken]], was run by an Adnerem named [[Etsero]], who had ties to the [[Crime lord/Legends|gangster]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] and to various [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] sympathizers.

Those Adnerem who left Adner often remained together in their steris and found group occupations. Steri away from their homeworld usually worked as management in large corporations, criminal organizations, or [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] agencies, such as the [[Interstellar Strike Force]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Adnerem were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of tall, slender, [[Diet/Legends|omnivorous]]<ref name="AE" /> [[Mammal/Legends|mammal]]<ref name="AE2" /> who normally stood between 1.8 and 2.2 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. Originally descended from a [[Scavenger/Legends|scavenger]]-[[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] [[Adnerem precursor species|precursor species]], they were [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] with many features in common with the [[Human/Legends|Human]] species, such as a bipedal gait, upright posture, and two different [[Gender/Legends|sexes]].

While they had a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] and a [[Nose/Legends|nose]] similar to those of Humans, they had distinctive triangle-shaped heads with no [[Ear/Legends|ears]], a narrow face and a wide [[Brain/Legends|brain]] pan, which supported a large lump on top of their skull. The lump was a firm, hollow [[echo chamber]], which functioned as an ear for the species, although Humans often mistkook it for a tumor. An Adnerem's skin was wrinkled in texture, dark gray in [[Color/Legends|color]] and completely [[Hair/Legends|hairless]] across their entire body, except for a single small vestigial patch of hair at the back of the [[Bone/Legends|skull]].<ref name="AE" />

The species possessed only four [[Limb/Legends|fingers]] on each of their hands, lacking an opposable thumb. The nails on each finger could grow incredibly long and sturdy. Adnerem's eyelids evolved to be narrow to protect their pair of [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]—which were usually light colors such as blue or green—against the twin [[Star/Legends|suns]] of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Adner]],<ref name="AE" />in the [[Yataga sector/Legends|Yataga sector]]<ref name="SW:EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="SW:EA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

==Society and culture==
===Steris society===
[[File:Adnerem_in_Bar.jpg|thumb|right|300px|An Adnerem stands in the foreground, turned toward the viewer, with several patrons sat around a bar in the background.|An Adnerem in a drinking establishment]]
Adnerem culture was based upon that of their ancient scavenger-hunter ancestors, who were semi-social and banded together in tribes that ranged in size from five to twenty individuals. The Adnerem themselves upheld the same group culture with a society based around socioeconomic social units of five to one hundred individuals, with the groups traditionally consisting of sixty-four adults whom were not necessarily related to one another genetically.<ref name="AE" />

These groups were known as [[steris]] individually, with the plural being steri and those within a steris known as its [[sterisi]]. The members of a steris lived together in a [[steris-house]], which normally took the form of a one or two floored disc-shaped building designed to evenly spread heat. Maintenance and housekeeping duties were shared amongst the sterisi, or another steris was employed to carry them out if the steri-house owners were wealthy enough. The building was divided into two areas, public and private, with individuals known to the steris allowed in the public section but none except the sterisi allowed in the private rooms.<ref name="AE" />

===Life choices===
Adnerem were born into a steris and raised by caregiving sterisi—not necessarily their [[Parent/Legends|parents]]—within the steri-house until the age of six [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old. At that point, the child was transferred into an [[education-steris]] to be broadly educated and assessed until the age of fourteen. At that point, the child chose an initial vocation. The next ten years of an Adnerem's life were spent training in the chosen occupation and paying back the steris for costs incurred during child-rearing. At the age of twenty-four, an Adnerem ceremonially left the steris and chose which steris to join as an adult.<ref name="AE" />

Most steri offered special incentives for their own sterisi to rejoin them, which resulted in roughly half of all Adnerem remaining with the steris they had been born into. Once the decision had been made, it was rare for an Adnerem to leave the chosen steris due to the fact that such a departure resulted in a forfeit of all assets associated with the abandoned steris and carried a slight stigma—although leaving was still seen as socially acceptable. Only Adnerem who had been promised far greater opportunity or status in another steris ever considered such a change.<ref name="AE" />

===Pure pragmatism===
The Adnerem devoutly followed a system of systematic pragmatism such that any Adnerem who wished to increase his or her position did so by improving his or her steris. That endeavor was mainly accomplished by increasing the steris's influence and [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|wealth]], usually through [[Merchant/Legends|trade]] with other steri. Most Adnerem worked hard at this task, although usually out of self-interest. Larger steri sometimes had smaller steri as clients whom they paid to gather resources for them, a position that the more powerful steri rarely abused.<ref name="AE" />

A less common way for steris's status to change was for them to engage in [[raid-war]]s. These were small conflicts between two or more steri, the goal of which was to weaken the enemy and so lower their status. Powerful steri engaged in such activities if they and another steris had conflicting goals, and smaller steri looking to make a name for themselves or to rise up against a powerful steris abusing its power might band together and engage in the conflicts. The battles were almost always short-lived and small in scale, with the objective never being total annihilation of the enemy, due to the fact that a weakened steris might prove useful later as an ally to the victor.<ref name="AE" />

Due to the steris system, the species had no social classes or social discrimination against either gender, and each individual was judged purely based on the status of the steris and his or her earned status within it. Adnerem therefore had particular difficulty understanding the concept of ranks and social positions used by other species. Despite this handicap, particularly wealthy Adnerem grew their nails to an extremely long length to show that they need not work. Female Adnerem often differentiated themselves from the males by growing their single patch of hair long and braiding it.<ref name="AE" /> The species generally wore [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] typical of many other humanoid species throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], particularly robes.<ref name="CLOTHES">In all four [[canon]]ical illustrations of Adnerem, they are shown wearing clothes similar to those of other humanoid species throughout ''Star Wars'' media. Of these images, three of the four show Adnerem in robes.</ref>

===Personality===
Although all personality types were found amongst the Adnerem, most were cool, collected and patient, and so appeared calm and dispassionate in nature. However, internally they were far more intense but simply did not show it. As a race they were also, in general, asocial and introverted, choosing to spend the majority of their spare time on their own or occasionally in very small groups of five or less. In larger groups, it was rare for any to speak, making public areas on their homeworld almost totally silent. On the other hand, two Adnerem alone could become as active and talkative as a group of ten individuals of another species.<ref name="AE" />

Occasionally, a pair within the same steris formed an incredibly close friendship known as a [[sterika]]. Sterika were non-[[Reproduction/Legends|sexual]] in nature, but the two individuals became so close that they considered themselves one entity. The reason for such pairings was unknown to the galaxy at large, although they usually occurred spontaneously after a period of stress for one or both of the Adnerem. Sterika were not sought out by Adnerem as other species sought love; however, Adnerem not in a sterika sometimes seemed to envy those who were. In total, only about ten percent of the Adnerem population at any one time was in a sterika; apart from such pairings, it was rare for Adnerem to become emotionally attached or form a strong bond with other individuals, whether of their own species or others. Such social distancing often led others to see Adnerem as remote and manipulative.<ref name="AE" />

===Outside influence===
Long-term immersion in [[Galactic community|galactic society]] eventually resulted in Adner's technology level catching up with the galactic standard. The Adnerem kept themselves separate from the galactic economy and remained financially isolated, investing heavily in remaining self-sufficient and independent from any kind of imports or investments offworld, with only specialty goods and information being imported in large quantities. The species' main income came from the entertainment industry they maintained on Adner, as well as a number of other [[Planet/Legends|planets]] that consisted primarily of pleasure and [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] establishments.<ref name="AE" />

For over two thousand years, the [[Casino/Legends|casinos]] on Adner were incredibly popular with offworld tourists, with hundreds of thousands flocking to the planet solely to visit them each year. The casinos, run by some of the most powerful steri, became adept at catering to the large number of visitors' needs, and employed mainly highly trained non-Adnerem as workers.<ref name="AE" />

==History==
Adnerem evolved from a species of primitive scavenger-hunters on the planet of Adner. Their ancestors originally lived in small tribes of five to twenty individuals. As the Adnerem developed socially, they remained highly group-oriented, and their groups became social units known as steri.<ref name="AE" /> Outsiders first reached the area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] where Adner was located between [[8000 BBY]] and [[5500 BBY/Legends|5500 BBY]].<ref name="EAD">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' shows the area of space in which the [[Yataga sector/Legends|Yataga sector]] is located; according to its grid coordinates of N-16 and its position in the system map of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] in the ''[[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion]]'', it lies within the green-shaded portion identified as regions of space first explored between [[8000 BBY]] and [[5500 BBY/Legends|5500 BBY]] in the map "Galactic Exploration".</ref> Members of the [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] species were the first to encounter the Adnerem on Adner. Nevertheless, offworld contact had little effect on their culture except for slowly introducing new technology. Adnerem culture kept the same steris-based system, and major historical events on Adner continued to be simple power shifts between different steri.<ref name="AE" />

==Adnerem in the galaxy==
[[File:Etsero.jpg|thumb|left|300px|alt=The Adnerem Etsero stands with his right arm across his stomach and his left elbow resting on his right hand, his left forearm raised up so that his clawed left hand lolls infront of his face.|Etsero, a wealthy Adnerem casino owner]]

===Etsero===
Adnerem generally preferred to remain at home on Adner and compete amongst themselves rather than the rest of the galaxy. Many Adnerem worked in the management of the casinos on Adner.<ref name="AE" /> One such individual was [[Etsero]], who lived during the reign of both the Galactic Empire and [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Etsero ran a casino named [[the Red Lisken]], which was one of the largest establishments on Adner. He came to be indebted to [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]],<ref name="WBC" /> a powerful [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] who ran his illicit empire from the planet of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref>

After the Hutt's death<ref name="WBC" /> at the [[Battle of the Great Pit of Carkoon]],<ref name="ROTJ" /> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="EC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref> the debt shifted to a separate party, who had Imperial loyalties. The situation led to the Adnerem being included on a document called ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]'', a list of wanted criminals and Imperials sought by [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]], a [[General/Legends|general]] in the New Republic.<ref name="WBC" /> Etsero was a known associate of fellow criminal, the Human [[Theol Dunoche]], who rescued two [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] women from [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] on the planet [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]] and brought them to work at Etsero's casino on Adner.<ref name="ND">[[No Disintegrations (West End Games)|''No Disintegrations'']]</ref>

===Other Adnerem===
Adnerem who left their homeworld almost always did so with other members of their steris. Some steri took up interstellar trading, running individual large cargo ships or fleets of smaller craft. A small number of steri hired themselves out to large galactic corporations as management teams on projects of small or medium size. One steris that left Adner set itself up as a mercenary company known as the [[Interstellar Strike Force]]. The steris acted as the command and control, while highly trained non-Adnerem were hired to act as the special operation units.<ref name="AE" />

Another [[Unidentified Adnerem mercenary|Adnerem]] at large became a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]]; the Adnerem made the acquaintance of [[Bitthævrian/Legends|Bitthævrian]] [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Quaal Tavier Catharius]] and joined the [[m'Yalfor'ac Order]]—an anti-Republic military order on the planet [[Guiteica/Legends|Guiteica]]—that Tavier led. The mercenary became part of a four-person leading committee for the order, as well as one of only two non-Bitthævrians in the organization.<ref name="AIR">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref>

[[File:Adnerem_GG11.jpg|thumb|right|300px|alt=An Adnerem in a black robe stands in an arched alley while a Herglic whispers to them and a Devaronian gestures to another being in front of them.|An Adnerem on the planet Tresidiss, a Herglic-run haven for criminals]]
Some steri turned their business skills to crime and operated small-scale criminal organizations, rarely larger in size than a few star systems. Such Adnerem criminals performed business quietly and eliminated threats and obstacles so as to draw as little attention as possible, often maximizing profit by having a broad range of small, easily hidable incomes instead of making big, risky attempts at a single large pay off, such as a bank robbery.<ref name="AE" /> One such Adnerem ended up on the planet [[Tresidiss]], a haven for criminals that was privately owned by [[Herglic/Legends|Herglic]] criminals who ran a large number of gambling establishments there and tolerated the illegal activities openly performed by the criminals who frequented the world.<ref name="GG11">''[[Galaxy Guide 11: Criminal Organizations]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Adnerem were first mentioned as a species in [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], a sourcebook written by [[Louis J. Prosperi]], which was released by [[West End Games]]<ref name="WBC" /> on [[June 1]], [[1993]]<ref name="BN">{{BN|url=w/star-wars-the-roleplaying-game-west-end-games-incorporated/1018175930|text=Star Wars the Roleplaying Game: Wanted by Cracken}}</ref> for their ''Star Wars'' [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition|roleplaying game]]. The image used for the Adnerem character in the book, Etsero,<ref name="WBC" /> was concept art drawn by artist [[Nilo Rodis-Jamero]] for the character [[Bib Fortuna/Legends|Bib Fortuna]] in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']].<ref name="AoROTJ">''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode VI: Return of the Jedi]]''</ref> The same sketch appeared in the [[1983]] concept art book ''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode VI: Return of the Jedi]]'',<ref name="AoROTJ" /> but was not selected for use in the film.<ref name="ROTJ" />

[[File:Monsters_and_Aliens_Adnerem.jpg|thumb|right|300px|alt=Two robes alien figures with brown skin and lumpy heads, one of which is the same image used for Etsero, but colored.|The use of the Adnerem image in ''Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas'']]
The sketch also appeared in<ref name="MaAfGL">''[[Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas]]''</ref> the [[November 1]], 1993<ref name="Waterstones">{{WebCite|url=https://www.waterstones.com/book/monsters-and-aliens-from-george-lucas/bob-carrau/9780810936409|text=Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas (Hardback)|work=[[Wikipedia:Watersteons|Waterstones.com]]|archivedate=22-12-08|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/xVqh3}}</ref> book ''[[Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas]]'', written by [[Bob Carrau]],<ref name="MaAfGL" /> alongside another Rodis-Jamero Bib Fortuna concept sketch.<ref name="AoROTJ" /> The images accompanied a holiday newsletter from a family named the [[Lingsnots]], in which they detailed the activities of the three Lingsnot children, [[Ussto Lingsnot|Ussto]], [[Miiliki-spoorr Lingsnot|Miiliki-spoorr]] and [[Ciption Lingsnot|Ciption]], over the past year. The [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] were never identified as Adnerem, and nothing in the letter referenced Adnerem culture—for example, the Lingsnots were a "family" instead of a "steris."<ref name="MaAfGL" /> Material from ''Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas'' is considered non-canon unless such material is referenced in later publications.<ref name="TT1">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Unless referred to by a source that is in-continuity, the information is non-continuity. With that said, if it makes sense for an author to incorporate some of that information into continuity, I wouldn't stop them.}}</ref> The image of the second Lingsnot character was used in the book ''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]'' to represent a character named [[Po Ruddle Lingsnot]], but his surname was the only link to the holiday letter, leaving the family's [[canon]]ical status unclear.<ref name="TISWU">''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]''</ref>

In [[November]], [[1996]], author [[Timothy S. O'Brien]] gave the species an entry in his article "[[Alien Encounters (article series)|Alien Encounters]]," included in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 11|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 11]], also published by West End Games. The article provided role playing statistics for the Adnerem, which showed they favored perception and knowledge, but were weak in technological aptitude.<ref name="AE" />
